signals.title: CHF — SNB Meeting + Positioning + Yields
signals.text: SNB Interest rates on sight deposits remain at 0.00% (up to threshold) and -0.25% (above threshold) as of today's SNB update, ahead of the 18 June meeting. Swiss 10Y bond yields have declined to 0.386% (from 0.406% on 15 June), indicating easing financial conditions. CHF COT LF net short −12,079 (long%=25.1%) represents an extreme speculative short position. This combination of unchanged policy rates, falling yields, and crowded short positioning creates significant short-squeeze risk if the SNB signals any hawkish shift or if risk-off flows emerge. USD/CHF at 0.7926. The SNB's official statement and press conference later today are the immediate catalysts.
evidence: SNB Interest rate on sight deposits: 0.00% / -0.25%

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Carry + Vol
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.27 has broken above the 160.00 level, a threshold that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings. The 296bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry premium (SOFR 3.69% vs TONA 0.73%) continues to sustain the bid, while USD/JPY IV at 7.2% vs HV30 at 3.3% (VRP +3.9%) indicates elevated options market hedging for upside. JPY COT LF net short −115,036 (long%=29.2%) is an extreme crowded short, increasing the risk of a sharp unwind if intervention occurs. Watch for official BoJ/MoF comments or a sustained move above 160.50.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −22,354 (long%=23.9%) indicates extreme speculative short positioning, diverging from the RBNZ's hawkish bias with a hike priced for the next meeting. Retail sentiment is 85% long, creating a strong institutional vs. retail divergence. The RBNZ's mildly accommodative real rate of -0.83% (policy 2.25% vs infl_exp 3.08%) suggests room for a hawkish surprise. If RBNZ delivers or signals a hike, a short-squeeze is likely, targeting 0.5900+. NZD/USD at 0.5812. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 20% hike priced) on 8 July is the key catalyst.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Retail Sentiment
signals.text: USD/CAD vs WTI Oil correlation has broken to −0.350 (vs 252d norm=−0.026, z=−1.63σ), indicating a decoupling where CAD is not strengthening with rising oil prices. WTI Oil is up +0.56% today, but USD/CAD is also up +0.17% at 1.4013. Retail sentiment is extremely short at 93% (7% long / 93% short), suggesting contrarian reversal risk if the correlation break persists. The 144bp OIS carry premium favors USD (SOFR 3.69% vs CORRA 2.25%), providing a structural bid for USD/CAD despite oil strength. Watch for a sustained move above 1.4050 to confirm the retail capitulation.
